text.title: Jenkins Basics Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree mapping observable signals (host, compliance, agent strategy) to a rule from 01-core-rules.xml.

decision-tree.root-question: Where does the repository live + what's the compliance posture?

conclusion.verdict: prefer github-actions-basics; do NOT introduce Jenkins
prefer gitlab-cicd; do NOT introduce Jenkins
use agent { kubernetes { yaml '...' } } with resources.requests + limits

branch.question: Docker daemon available on agent VMs?

conclusion.verdict: use agent { docker { image '...' args '...' } } single-container
use agent { label 'linux' } with labelled long-lived agents; NEVER agent any or controller
install JCasC, export current config to jenkins.yaml, commit to source control before any further change
replace literal with credentials() / withCredentials() binding; rotate the leaked secret

text.example: host=self-hosted → cluster=true → use kubernetes agent with explicit resource limits → enforces kubernetes-agent rule.
